projects.title: Project 1: Computer Evolution Timeline
projects.description: Create a visual timeline in your notebook showing how computers have changed from 1950 to 2025. Include at least 10 major milestones: ENIAC, first personal computer, Apple II, IBM PC, Windows, Internet, Pentium, smartphones, AI, modern laptops. Draw pictures and write 2-3 sentences for each.

projects.title: Project 2: Binary & Number Systems
projects.description: Learn about binary numbers (0s and 1s). Write your name in binary using ASCII codes in your notebook. Convert these numbers to binary: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25. Also learn about hex codes used in colors (#FF0000 = red). Find 5 color hex codes and explain them.

projects.title: Project 3: Input-Output Devices Collection
projects.description: List and draw 15 different input devices (keyboard, mouse, scanner, microphone, touch screen, etc.) and 10 output devices (printer, speaker, monitor, headphones, etc.) in your notebook. For each, write what it does and an example of when you would use it.

projects.title: Project 4: Software Types Guide
projects.description: Create a guide in your notebook explaining different types of software: System Software (Windows, macOS, Linux), Application Software (MS Office, Photoshop, Games), Programming Software (Python, Java). For each type give 3 examples and explain what they do in simple words.

projects.title: Project 5: Computer Care Checklist
projects.description: Create a page in your notebook with a checklist for computer/phone care: 1) Update software regularly, 2) Use strong passwords, 3) Install antivirus, 4) Backup important files, 5) Clean the screen and keyboard, 6) Don't click suspicious links, 7) Log out of shared accounts. Draw icons or pictures for each item.

content.materials: Notebook, colors, sketch pens, textbook for reference. All work should be done in your notebook!
content.submission: Bring back your holiday notebook with: 1) Computer evolution timeline, 2) Binary numbers practice, 3) I/O devices list (25 items), 4) Software types guide, 5) Computer care checklist.

grading.average: Completes all 5 projects with neat presentation and some drawings.
grading.bright: Adds extra research, neat diagrams, creative presentation, and colorful decorations.

content.note: Prepare well for Class 10 boards! Use this time to understand computer fundamentals! 📧 You can email me anytime at venkatavishnu.skksv@gmail.com or message me on Instagram @comaecod. I will surely reply!
